payload.title: Capital Program Committee Meeting
payload.description: Capital Program Committee Meeting materials including minutes from April 27, 2026, President's Update, Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 update, 125th Street Subway Extension feasibility study, Interborough Express update, IEC report, procurement actions, and capital program status reports.

payload.description: Joint venture consisting of Hard Rock Casino and Steve Cohen, owner of the New York Mets. Awarded a downstate gaming license by the New York State Gaming Commission.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Queens Future, a joint venture consisting of Hard Rock Casino and Steve Cohen, owner of the New York Mets, was awarded one of three downstate gaming licenses by the New York State Gaming Commission.

payload.description: Joint venture awarded contract for design and engineering services for accessibility improvements on NYCT lines

evidence_refs.source_quote: Award of a publicly advertised and competitively solicited contract with EXP-Urbahn Joint Venture (Contract No. D81760)

payload.status: active, on schedule and within budget
payload.description: Extension of the Second Avenue Subway north to East Harlem. Four major contracts with combined budget of $6.968 billion. Four contracts: Contract 1 (utility relocation), Contract 2 (design-build tunnel), Contract 3 (open trench, five blocks 60-ft deep, 105th-110th Streets), Contract 4 (station interiors/systems). Forecasted revenue service date September 2032.

payload.status: design development and environmental review underway
payload.description: New transit connection linking Brooklyn and Queens along existing 14-mile freight rail corridor between Sunset Park and Jackson Heights. 18 stations, connections to 17 subway lines, 50+ bus routes, and LIRR. Light rail selected as preferred mode in 2023. Design RFP issued 2024. Included in MTA Capital Plan 2025, not yet fully funded. Environmental review/DEIS anticipated later this year (2026).

payload.status: feasibility study completed, environmental review started
payload.description: Proposed extension of the Q train westward from its planned Phase 2 terminus in East Harlem to Broadway on Manhattan's West Side along 125th Street. Would serve more than 150,000 daily riders. Feasibility study concluded it is feasible to construct tunnel and future stations. Environmental review process has begun, completion expected mid-to-late next year.

evidence_refs.source_quote: could serve more than 150,000 daily riders
the study concluded that it is feasible to construct a tunnel and future stations along 125th Street

tool_args.label: Mets-Willets Point Station State of Good Repair Design Services

payload.project_name: Mets-Willets Point Station State of Good Repair Design Services
payload.description: Design Agreement with Queens Future, LLC for STV to provide state of good repair inspection and design services at Mets-Willets Point Station on the IRT Flushing Line. Includes updated inspection report, conceptual design, 60% design and 100% design documents. Queens Future committed to fully ADA-accessible station improvements.
payload.location: Mets-Willets Point Station, IRT Flushing Line, Queens

location_normalized.raw_text: Mets-Willets Point Station, IRT Flushing Line, Queens

tool_args.label: Replacement of East End Bridges - LIRR Montauk Branch

payload.project_name: Replacement of East End Bridges - LIRR Montauk Branch
payload.description: Design-build contract to replace three bridges on LIRR Montauk Branch: River Avenue and Sagg Road bridges in Southampton, and Cranberry Hole Road bridge in East Hampton, NY. Awarded to RCC/GCCOM A Joint Venture LLC for $36,589,000. Duration 721 calendar days.
payload.location: Southampton and East Hampton, New York

location_normalized.raw_text: Southampton and East Hampton, New York

tool_args.label: RFK Fleet Garage Exit Corridor Repairs

payload.project_name: RFK Fleet Garage Exit Corridor Repairs
payload.description: Rehabilitation and repair of exit corridors and miscellaneous spaces at MTA Bridges and Tunnels' RFK Fleet Garage at Randall's Island. Modification for additional civil, mechanical and electrical work, with 12-month substantial completion extension.

tool_args.label: Installation of Bike Racks at 14 Metro-North Stations

payload.project_name: Installation of Bike Racks at 14 Metro-North Stations
payload.description: Installation of bicycle racks and related work at 14 Metro-North Stations on the Harlem, Hudson, Pascack Valley, and Port Jervis Lines. Modification for additional sidewalk and retaining wall at Hastings-on-Hudson Station due to flooding damage.

MTA Capital Program Committee Meeting. Agenda included summary of actions, approval of minutes, public comments, committee work plan, President's Update, Agency Initiatives Update, C&D Safety Report, Capital Program Status Report, C&D Procurements, and Action item on Mets-Willets Station.

payload.claim_text: The Interborough Express will link Brooklyn and Queens along an existing 14-mile freight rail corridor between Sunset Park and Jackson Heights, adding passenger rail service while preserving freight operations. The plan includes 18 stations and connections to 17 subway lines, more than 50 bus routes, and the Long Island Rail Road.
payload.subject: Interborough Express
payload.description: Project description and connectivity for the IBX light rail project

payload.claim_text: Design development for IBX resulted in several project refinements: the originally proposed street-running segment has been eliminated; the Atlantic Avenue station in East New York has been relocated closer to Broadway Junction; the proposed Sutter Avenue station has been removed to reduce travel times.
payload.subject: Interborough Express
payload.description: Changes to IBX design based on public feedback

payload.claim_text: The feasibility study identified a rare opportunity to synchronize the 125th Street Subway Extension with Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 by extending the same tunnel boring machines westward along 125th Street toward Broadway, potentially saving taxpayers hundreds of millions of dollars.
payload.subject: 125th Street Subway Extension
payload.description: Cost and schedule efficiency opportunity by extending TBMs from SAS Phase 2

payload.claim_text: The principal challenge facing the Interborough Express is securing sufficient funding for construction. Initial phase of civil construction estimated at approximately $3 billion for essential bridge, retaining wall, and right-of-way improvements.
payload.subject: Interborough Express
payload.description: Funding status and initial phase cost estimate for IBX

payload.claim_text: The Park Avenue Viaduct project achieved substantial completion of all original project work south of 125th Street. Work north of 125th Street is expected to conclude later this year, with full project completion anticipated in 2027. The project remains four years ahead of its original schedule.
payload.subject: Park Avenue Viaduct
payload.description: Milestone update and ahead-of-schedule status for the Park Avenue Viaduct project

tool_args.label: C&D President's Update
tool_args.raw_text: Mark Roche, Deputy Chief Development Officer, Delivery, presented on behalf of Jamie Torres-Springer

payload.description: MTA C&D presented President's Update at the May 20, 2026 Capital Program Committee Meeting

Prior Capital Program Committee Meeting called to order at 12:05 PM by Chairman Lieber. Included public comments period (6 speakers), work plan review, approval of March 23, 2026 minutes, President's Report, Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 update, Interborough Express update, IEC Report, and procurement actions.

Series of six public workshops beginning the following evening (from May 20) and continuing through end of May to gather community feedback on IBX station design concepts. Part of broader outreach strategy including formal hearings on draft environmental impact statement.

payload.event_name: Substantial Completion - Park Avenue Viaduct South of 125th Street
payload.description: Substantial completion of all original project work south of 125th Street, including track, signal, and systems installations. Project remains four years ahead of original schedule.
